This is evident from the title "Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: Long-Term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial" and the abstract which mentions "ADT in combination with docetaxel". Also, the purpose states "Docetaxel added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) significantly increases the longevity of some patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer."

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: 397 in the ADT plus docetaxel arm.
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: The study abstract states that 790 patients were equally randomized to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone. The full number and percentage is not explicitly stated in the abstract, but can be calculated from the total number of patients and the study design. Page 4 states that 397 patients were in the ADT plus docetaxel arm. Page 4 also states that 263 patients (66.2%) in the combination arm had high-volume disease.

Median OS (mo) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: In this prospectively defined subgroup, with a median follow-up of 53.7 months, there was a median OS benefit of 16.8 months (median OS, 51.2 v 34.4 months; HR for death in the combination arm, 0.63; 95% CI, 0.50 to 0.79; P , .001).

*   **Page 1, Abstract:** '...either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles...'
*   **Page 3, Procedures:** '...randomly assigned at a one-to-one ratio to ADT alone versus ADT plus docetaxel at a dose of 75 mg/m² every 3 weeks for up to six cycles without daily prednisone.'

To extract the value, look for instances of 'ADT plus docetaxel' or 'androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) in combination with docetaxel'.
